Mechanically Stable and Tunable Photoactivated Peptide‐Based Hydrogels for Soft Tissue Adhesion (Adv. Funct. Mater. 27/2026)
Alex Ross, Daniel Nguyen, Aidan J. MacAdam, German A. Mercado Salazar, Micaela Gianetti, Ramis Ileri, Marc Ruel, Erik J. Suuronen, Emilio I. Alarcon

e74831 | First Published: 03 April 2026

Biomimetic Peptides

Bioinspired synthetic collagen-like peptide hydrogels offer the advantages of both natural and synthetic biomaterials. In their Research Article (10.1002/adfm.202529084), Erik J. Suuronen, Emilio I. Alarcon, and co-workers show that, by combining self-assembly with light-triggered crosslinking, these tunable materials achieve bonding strength comparable to commercial adhesives like LiquiBand. Additionally, their cytocompatibility and biodegradability make them a robust platform for soft tissue repair and wound closure.

    Inside Front Cover

    Free Access
    Tuning Radiative Properties of Multiscale Porous Transparent Silica Aerogels for Efficient Solar Interfacial Evaporation (Adv. Funct. Mater. 27/2026)
    Shiteng Li, Jiawen Zhang, Yuzhu Chen, Shang Liu, Wenjun Lu, Shuai Deng, Meng Lin

    e74833 | First Published: 03 April 2026

    Solar Interfacial Evaporation

    The cover illustrates a greenhouse-selective, transparent silica aerogel used as an optical and thermal management cover for solar interfacial evaporation. Sunlight is transmitted through the aerogel, while the infrared thermal radiation emitted from the hot interface is effectively suppressed, thereby reducing radiative heat loss. The magnified inset highlights the porous pearl-chain nanostructure of the silica aerogel, in which minimized nanoscale scattering centers ensure low light scattering and high transmittance, ultimately enhancing the solar evaporation performance. More information can be found in the Research Article by Shuai Deng, Meng Lin, and co-workers (10.1002/adfm.202528287).

      Inside Back Cover

      Free Access
      Adaptive 4D‐Printed Vascular Stents With Low‐Temperature‐Activated and Intelligent Deployment (Adv. Funct. Mater. 27/2026)
      Yannan Li, Yifan Pan, Chikahiro Imashiro, Chaolun Xu, Jianxian He, Jingao Xu, Kewei Song, Ze Zhang, Chen Gao, Junbo Jiang, Runhuai Yang, Kayo Hirose, Shinjiro Umezu

      e74832 | First Published: 03 April 2026

      4D-Printed Vascular Stents

      In their Research Article (10.1002/adfm.202521468), Kayo Hirose, Shinjiro Umezu, and coworkers fabricate a micro-stereolithography 4D-printed stent via ultraviolet photocrosslinking of a polycaprolactone (PCL)-based material system. Diethyl phthalate (DEP) is incorporated to regulate the shape memory threshold of the manufactured structure, enabling intelligent deployment at physiological temperature with advanced thermoresponsive performance, mechanical compliance, and biocompatibility for personalized minimally invasive vascular therapy.

        Back Cover

        Free Access
        Where Most Frameworks Degrade: Flexible Bimetallic Phosphonate Crystals as pH‐Universal Supercapacitor Electrodes (Adv. Funct. Mater. 27/2026)
        Tim Müller, Jean G. A. Ruthes, Biswajit Battacharya, Dorothee Silbernagl, Patrik Tholen, Aysenur Limon, Gülsüm Kinik, Christoph Janiak, Markus Suta, Franziska Emmerling, Volker Presser, Gündoğ Yücesan

        e74834 | First Published: 03 April 2026

        pH-Universal Supercapacitor Electrodes

        Redox-active [Cu(2,2′-bpy)VO(O3PC6H5)2] forms flexible, bimetallic phosphonate crystals that serve as supercapacitor electrodes. In aqueous electrolytes at pH 4 (H3PO4) and pH 10 (NaOH), the material delivers about 140 F/g and remains stable over 10,000 cycles. Retaining crystallinity after exposure to pH 2-12 enables durable operation in aqueous environments. More information can be found in the Research Article by Franziska Emmerling, Volker Presser, Gündoğ Yücesan, and co-workers (10.1002/adfm.202600026).

          Issue Information

          Free Access
          Issue Information

          e74835 | First Published: 03 April 2026

            Review

            Pathway Engineering in Li–CO2 Batteries: Mitigating Energy Barriers via Optimized 4e−/3CO2 and 2e−/2CO2 Redox Chemistry
            Wenqing Ma, Tianzhen Jian, Yuyan Wang, Jianping Ma, Haiyang Gao, Xianhong Li, Siyu Liu, Mingjuan Gao, Xiangping Chen, Lishan Yang, Hong Liu

            e24344 | First Published: 15 January 2026

            This review outlines pathway engineering in Li–CO2 batteries, focusing on both the optimized 4e−/3CO2 and alternative 2e−/2CO2 redox routes. By reducing energy barriers and improving reversibility through catalyst design, electrolyte optimization, external field assistance, and operational innovations, these approaches enhance battery performance and support CO2 utilization in energy storage.

              Stimuli‐Responsive Liquid Crystal Elastomers: From Materials to Applications
              Jiao Liu, Hao-Yi Jiang, Jing-Qi Tian, Yuqi Tang, Chi-Bo Feng, Xin-Yu Zhou, Zhi-Jun Huang, Bing-Xiang Li, Yan-Qing Lu, Quan Li

              e24606 | First Published: 03 December 2025

              The state-of-the-art advances in stimuli-responsive LCEs are reviewed, including thermo-responsive LCEs, photo-responsive LCEs, electro-responsive LCEs, chemical-responsive LCEs, magnetic-responsive LCEs, humidity-responsive LCEs, radio-frequency-responsive LCEs, ultrasonic-responsive LCEs, and photothermal-responsive LCEs that have contributed to the resurgence in LCE research. Furthermore, the applications of these LCEs in information encryption, force sensors, and material transportation are demonstrated, which have significant potential for further development of the next-generation advanced functional materials.

                Open Access
                Spectrally Tunable 2D Material‐Based Infrared Photodetectors for Intelligent Optoelectronics
                Junheon Ha, Yingshan Ma, Yong Nam An, Sung-Un An, Hyeon Hak Jung, Suvi-Tuuli Varjamo, Jiwon Yoo, Junho Min, Hanvit Kim, Faisal Ahmed, Sang Hoon Chae, Young Min Song, Weiwei Cai, Tawfique Hasan, Zhipei Sun, Dong-Ho Kang, Hyeon-Jin Shin, Yunyun Dai, Hoon Hahn Yoon

                e19542 | First Published: 02 December 2025

                Intelligent optoelectronics through spectral engineering of 2D material-based infrared photodetectors.

                  Decoding Hard Carbon Microstructure for Fast‐Charging Lithium‐Ion Batteries
                  Lian Chen, Fan Li, Feng Wang, Zhengshuai Bai, Yanyan Zhang, Yuxin Tang

                  e19895 | First Published: 28 November 2025

                  Hard carbon is a promising anode material for fast-charging lithium-ion batteries (LIBs). Here, it is aimed to comprehensively decode the microstructure of hard carbon for fast-charging LIBs from the commercialization perspective, including the design strategies of active sites and ion/electron transport, and solid electrolyte interface engineering.

                    Self‐Assembling Peptide‐Based Materials for Therapy of Inflammatory Diseases
                    Jintao Shen, Guizhi Shen, Tianyu Li, Chaojuan Yang, Ruirui Xing, Xuehai Yan

                    e21597 | First Published: 27 November 2025

                    Self-assembling peptide-based materials offer versatile structural and functional platforms for the precise regulation of inflammatory microenvironments. By integrating molecular design, tunable assembly, and targeted therapeutic functions, these systems address diverse inflammatory diseases and demonstrate significant potential for translation into advanced biomedical applications.

                      Clay‐Enabled (quasi) Solid‐State Electrolytes for Metal Batteries: Toward Safe, Sustainable, and High‐Energy Storage
                      Zhangkuo Han, Shusen Zhou, Di Jiang, Jiahan Zheng, Huiying Hao, Jie Xing, Jingjing Dong, Hao Liu, Libing Liao

                      e23711 | First Published: 28 November 2025

                      This review systematically elucidates how clay minerals enable high-performance (quasi) solid-state electrolytes through structural ion-transport mechanisms, multicomponent interactions, and interface engineering, highlighting low-cost, high-performance design strategies, clay–polymer coupling effects, and future directions for safe, scalable solid-state metal batteries.

                        RESEARCH ARTICLE

                        Mechanically Stable and Tunable Photoactivated Peptide‐Based Hydrogels for Soft Tissue Adhesion
                        Alex Ross, Daniel Nguyen, Aidan J. MacAdam, German A. Mercado Salazar, Micaela Gianetti, Ramis Ileri, Marc Ruel, Erik J. Suuronen, Emilio I. Alarcon

                        e29084 | First Published: 27 December 2025

                        A collagen-like peptide hydrogel platform is developed using supramolecular self-assembly and light-triggered crosslinking. It creates mechanically stable, tunable hydrogels with cytocompatibility and biodegradability, making them potential soft tissue adhesives.

                          Tuning Radiative Properties of Multiscale Porous Transparent Silica Aerogels for Efficient Solar Interfacial Evaporation
                          Shiteng Li, Jiawen Zhang, Yuzhu Chen, Shang Liu, Wenjun Lu, Shuai Deng, Meng Lin

                          e28287 | First Published: 22 January 2026

                          Guided by a multiscale modeling framework, transparent silica aerogels are engineered by tuning the mean scattering center radius and bulk density. A 5 mm-thick aerogel cover achieves about 95.2% solar-weighted transmittance while maintaining thermal blocking, and the optimized design increases the freshwater production of a 10-stage solar interfacial evaporation device by 19.3%.

                            Open Access
                            Adaptive 4D‐Printed Vascular Stents With Low‐Temperature‐Activated and Intelligent Deployment
                            Yannan Li, Yifan Pan, Chikahiro Imashiro, Chaolun Xu, Jianxian He, Jingao Xu, Kewei Song, Ze Zhang, Chen Gao, Junbo Jiang, Runhuai Yang, Kayo Hirose, Shinjiro Umezu

                            e21468 | First Published: 15 January 2026

                            Microarchitected coronary artery stents were fabricated using a polycaprolactone (PCL)-based shape memory polymer (SMP) composite via projection micro-stereolithography (PµSL) 4D printing. By incorporating diethyl phthalate (DEP) as a plasticizer, the thermal transition temperature (Ttran) was modulated to about 37°C, enabling rapid and autonomous shape recovery under physiological conditions without external heating.

                              Open Access
                              Where Most Frameworks Degrade: Flexible Bimetallic Phosphonate Crystals as pH‐Universal Supercapacitor Electrodes
                              Tim Müller, Jean G. A. Ruthes, Biswajit Battacharya, Dorothee Silbernagl, Patrik Tholen, Aysenur Limon, Gülsüm Kinik, Christoph Janiak, Markus Suta, Franziska Emmerling, Volker Presser, Gündoğ Yücesan

                              e00026 | First Published: 04 February 2026

                              Plastically flexible single crystals of the bimetallic phosphonate framework [Cu(2,2′-bpy)VO(O3PC6H5)2] combine mechanical adaptability with robust pseudocapacitive charge storage. The material delivers about 140 Fg−1 at pH 4 and pH 10 and remains stable across pH 2-12, enabling energy storage under comparatively mild electrolyte conditions.

                                Research Article

                                Open Access
                                Solar Heating Enhanced Selective Recovery of Metal Ions Through Flowing Electrodes Enabled by Hybrid Carbon Nanostructures
                                Ziquan Wang, Jiaqi Chen, Qiuru Lin, Mingxing Liang, Bo Yang, Yantao Chen, Minjun Kim, Yusuke Yamauchi, Stephen J. Eichhorn, Zhiqun Lin, Magdalena Titirici, Libo Deng

                                e20231 | First Published: 24 November 2025

                                A new electrochemical system based on a microporous hybrid of carbon nanoplatelets and nanotubes to selectively capture Ni2+ from wastewater is constructed. The system temperature rises rapidly when irradiated with sunlight, which enhances the Ni2+ removal rate by 250% and the selectivity by 53%, and the energy consumption is also reduced by 51%.

                                  Local Charge Enhancement at Heterojunction Interfaces Caused by High‐Density Dislocations for Improving Photocatalytic H2O2 Production
                                  Chen Li, Jiawei Song, Xinyu Guo, Rui Zhang, Peijie Ma, Kun Zheng

                                  e25385 | First Published: 20 November 2025

                                  The construction of a heterojunction by coating layered CoO2 with high-density stacking faults with g-C3N4 enables highly efficient photocatalytic H2O2 production. The synergistic interplay between stacking fault-induced localized charge enrichment and interfacial charge transfer reconstructs the electronic structure of the g-C3N4 shell, significantly enhancing the separation efficiency of photogenerated carriers and the activity of 2e− ORR.

                                    Open Access
                                    Mechanical Properties of Architected Polymer Lattice Materials: A Comparative Study of Additive Manufacturing and CAD Using FEM and µ‐CT
                                    Oliver Walker, Thierry Roland, Damien Favier, Antoine Egele, Michael Heymann, Cosima Stubenrauch

                                    e12520 | First Published: 24 November 2025

                                    This study examines how pore shape and manufacturing-induced deviations affect the mechanical properties of 3D-printed lattice materials with constant porosity. Combining µ-CT analysis, FEM, and compression testing, the authors show that structural imperfections reduce stiffness and strength, while bulk material inhomogeneities probably enhance mechanical performance. Pore shape governs stress distribution and deformation, offering a key parameter for mechanical property tuning.

                                      Open Access
                                      Unusual Swelling Behavior of Hydrogels Modified with Spiropyran as Appendage or Crosslinker
                                      Michael M. Lerch, Ankita Shastri, Thomas B. H. Schroeder, Amos Meeks, Shucong Li, Anna V. Shneidman, Michael Aizenberg, Joanna Aizenberg

                                      e18243 | First Published: 24 November 2025

                                      Not so innocent after all—spiropyran crosslinkers in methylenebisacrylamide-crosslinked poly(acrylamide-co-acrylic acid) hydrogels increase crosslinking density, but also, counterintuitively, increase swelling. Charge complexation, cooperative chemo-mechanical effects, and aggregation may explain these observations. Understanding the underlying mechanism may help to engineer more complex hydrogel actuation behaviors.

                                        Open Access
                                        A Termite‐Inspired Alternative to Cement
                                        Oren Regev, Peter Fratzl, Emeline Raguin, Markus Antonietti

                                        e16256 | First Published: 29 November 2025

                                        A termite-inspired composite of clay, cellulose, and lignin forms a dense fibrous network with concrete-like strength (32 MPa) and superior elasticity, processed at ambient temperature.

                                          Editor's Choice

                                          Conformation‐Induced Dipole Polarization Engineering in Hierarchical Vinylene‐Linked Covalent Organic Frameworks for Enhanced Photocatalytic H2O2 Production
                                          Chao Yang, Wenning Ma, Jie Zhang, Wenbo Wei, Zhuoran Kuang, Hongping Zhou, Qi-Long Zhu

                                          e26519 | First Published: 22 November 2025

                                          Two hierarchical vinylene-linked covalent organic frameworks (COFs) featuring either symmetrical or asymmetrical configurations are successfully synthesized, of which the local electronic structures can be directionally controlled. The COF with an asymmetrical configuration demonstrates improved dipole polarization, expedited exciton separation, and enhanced charge-transfer efficiency, ultimately leading to superior photocatalytic performance for H2O2 production in pure water.

                                            Ultralow Lattice Thermal Conductivity Induced by Unique Hierarchical Rotational Dynamics in Lightweight Cyanide‐Bridged Framework Materials
                                            Zhunyun Tang, Xiaoxia Wang, Jin Li, Chaoyu He, Mingxing Chen, Chao Tang, Tao Ouyang

                                            e26344 | First Published: 05 December 2025

                                            The combination of hierarchical vibrations and rotational dynamics in cyanide-bridged framework materials leads to ultralow lattice thermal conductivity, unveiling a novel mechanism for the effective suppression of thermal transport. The study provides crucial theoretical guidance for discovering lightweight materials with ultralow thermal conductivity and applying them in thermoelectric or thermal insulation.

                                              Open Access
                                              From Mechanics to Electronics: Influence of ALD Interlayers on the Multiaxial Electro‐Mechanical Behavior of Metal–Oxide Bilayers
                                              Johanna Byloff, Vivek Devulapalli, Daniele Casari, Thomas E. J. Edwards, Claus O. W. Trost, Megan J. Cordill, Shuhel Altaf Husain, Pierre-Olivier Renault, Damien Faurie, Barbara Putz

                                              e26343 | First Published: 27 November 2025

                                              Ultrathin AlOxHy interlayers between aluminum films and polymer substrates significantly improve electro-mechanical properties of flexible thin film systems. By precisely controlling interlayer thickness using atomic layer deposition, this study identifies an optimal interlayer thickness of 5–10 nm that enhances ductility and delays cracking. Thinner and thicker interlayers cause brittleness. This approach enables tailoring properties for flexible electronics or space applications.

                                                Open Access
                                                Scalable Thermal Engineering via Femtosecond Laser‐Direct‐Written Phononic Nanostructures
                                                Hiroki Hamma, Roman Anufriev, Kazuyoshi Fushinobu, Masahiro Nomura, Byunggi Kim

                                                e25269 | First Published: 03 December 2025

                                                This study demonstrates that femtosecond laser-induced periodic surface structures (fs-LIPSS) can function as phononic metasurfaces, reducing thermal conductivity below the plain thin-film limit. Phonon Monte Carlo analysis reveals that the periodic structures restrict phonon mean free paths. This approach offers a new avenue for scalable thermal engineering, delivering orders-of-magnitude higher throughput than conventional lithography.

                                                  Shear‐Induced CROSS (Cellular RedOx Spreading Shield) Assembly Sustains Neurotrophic Extracellular Vesicle Production for Functional Neural Networks
                                                  Ryan C. Miller, Sehong Kang, Jason Wang, Kai-Yu Huang, Jonghwi Lee, Young Jun Kim, Hee-Sun Han, Hyunjoon Kong

                                                  e22252 | First Published: 23 November 2025

                                                  This article introduces an innovative droplet microfluidic technique for creating drug-crystal-loaded microgels that facilitate potent extracellular vesicle production from mesenchymal stromal cells. By mitigating oxidation propagation, these microgels boost neurogenesis and establish functional neuronal connections in microphysiological systems, enabling bio-computation and neurobiology modeling. Additionally, this work combines fluorescent microscopy with graph theory to gain spatial understanding of correlated neuronal firing events.

                                                    Bioderived Ellagate‐Based Metal–Organic Framework Photocatalysts for Photoenhanced Uranium Sequestration
                                                    Yun-Han Mao, Qun-Yan Wu, Yu-Qian Zhong, Jian-Hui Lan, Hong-Bin Qi, Wei Jin, Zhi-Wei Huang, Kong-Qiu Hu, Wei-Qun Shi, Lei Mei

                                                    e25346 | First Published: 20 November 2025

                                                    A new kind of bioderived ellagic acid (EA)-based thorium-organic framework materials is proposed as both excellent adsorbents and photocatalysts for uranium removal, among which the robust Th-EA with π–π stacking shows 229.1 mg·g−1 U(VI) adsorption at pH 5 via ion-exchange, and is able to greatly improve uranyl removal rate to 96.2% by transforming adsorbed uranyl into insoluble studtite phase under visible light irradiation.

                                                      Unlocking Li‐Ions from “Cages” and “Shackles” in PVDF‐HFP Solid‐State Electrolyte through Synergistic Pinning Effect and Competitive Coordination
                                                      Shiyuan Zhang, Ying Liu, Hong Teng, Aotian Zhang, Tianzong Ma, Zhentong Kong, Nan Zhang, Shuling Qin, Huiyan Wang, Haoyuan Zhu, Ruonan Jing, Chungang Wang, Haiming Xie, Liqun Sun

                                                      e25220 | First Published: 28 November 2025

                                                      This work presents a simple and universal synergistic strategy of combining pinning effects and competitive coordination to liberate Li-ions from the confining “cages” and “shackles” within poly (vinylidene fluoride) (PVDF) -based electrolytes. Through leveraging custom WO3 nanosheets and the coordination regulator N-methylacetamide (N-MA), effective pathways are created that accelerate Li+ transport within the electrolyte. This approach provides guiding principles for designing and precisely regulating of PVDF-based electrolytes.

                                                        Synergistically Promoting Polaron Hopping via Element Doping and Photothermal Effect for High‐Efficient Photoelectrochemical Water Splitting
                                                        Wenfeng Li, Guocheng Lv, Meng Liu, Fanyue Zhao, Zetian He, Guihong Li, Wenping Wang, Daimei Chen, Libing Liao

                                                        e23344 | First Published: 02 December 2025

                                                        Mo doping and the introduction of Ni2P co-catalysts passivate the surface state of BiVO4, mitigate Fermi level pinning, and enhance quasi-Fermi level splitting. The Ni2P photothermal effect lowers the electron polaron hopping energy barriers, releases trapped polaron, and brings the polaron state of BiVO4 closer to the band edge, which promotes electron polaron hopping, accelerates carrier transport, and thus accelerates water oxidation kinetics.

                                                          Open Access
                                                          Using the Photostationary State of Arylazopyrazoles to Control Phase Transitions of Liquid Crystals
                                                          Tobias Thiele, Kristina Hrybenko, Christoph Wölper, Michael Giese

                                                          e21709 | First Published: 05 December 2025

                                                          A series of new arylazopyrazole photoswitches is designed as dopants for liquid crystalline materials. Unprecedented, the distribution of photoisomers at the photostationary state upon irradiation with light of specific wavelengths (365, 460, 520 nm) is used to control the liquid crystalline phase transitions under isothermal conditions, including nematic, smectic, and isotropic phases.

                                                            Quantum Geometric Photocurrents in Anisotropic Moiré Superlattices: Enabling High‐Dimensional Structured Light Recognition and Compact Computer Vision
                                                            Xueyan Cui, Xiuhua Xie, Ying Song, Shanqing Li, Binghui Li, Zekun Qu, Wei Chen, Yuwei Shan, Zhen Cheng, Jinluo Cheng, Shichen Su, Shuangpeng Wang, Jishan Liu, Hongyu Chen

                                                            e11036 | First Published: 23 November 2025

                                                            This study demonstrates that twist-angle engineering in anisotropic ReS2 moiré superlattices tailors non-trivial quantum geometry, resulting in pronounced nonlinear photoresponses. Second harmonic generation and photocurrent measurements reveal a twist-angle-dependent modulation of quantum geometric properties, driving exceptional circular photogalvanic effects and polarization-/wavelength-sensitive responses. Vortex-beam-based image recognition further highlights the potential for advanced optoelectronic device applications.

                                                              Crossbow Bioreactors for Studying the Effects of Time‐Varying Mechanical Preload and Afterload on Engineered Cardiac Tissues
                                                              Abbigail Helfer, Nicholas Strash, Marisa Patsy, Nenad Bursac

                                                              e19601 | First Published: 05 December 2025

                                                              A novel “crossbow” bioreactor is developed to study how different types of mechanical load influence engineered tissues. Progressive increases in mechanical preload, afterload, or both, with time of culture, produced distinct structural, functional, and molecular effects on engineered heart tissues termed “cardiobundles.” This tunable platform will enable new studies of heart development and pathophysiology in vitro.

                                                                2D MOF/COF Heterojunction for Efficient Photothermal Synergistic Catalysis of CO2 Reduction
                                                                Nan-Nan Zhang, Yan Sun, Xin-Ran Liu, Rong Yin, Ya Wang, Shuang-Yu Wang, Jin-Zhi Wei, GuiLing Zhang, Hong Dong, Feng-Ming Zhang

                                                                e23743 | First Published: 22 November 2025

                                                                A shared-linker bonded 2D MOF/COF heterojunction with S-scheme charge transfer pathway is designed, enabling efficient photothermal synergistic catalysis without adding additional photosensitizers and achieving record-high CO evolution under visible near-infrared (Vis–NIR) light in CAT-1/COF-316.

                                                                  Homologous Mott–Schottky Electrocatalysts Enable Record Cycling Stability in Zn‐Air Battery and Water Splitting
                                                                  Rong Xin, Hongqing Zhao, Yijiang Liu, Ying Yuan, Shuzhi Liu, Dan Li, Mei Yang, Bei Liu, Shujiang Ding, Zhiqun Lin

                                                                  e24205 | First Published: 24 November 2025

                                                                  A robust trifunctional electrocatalyst, comprising FeNi/FeNiO Mott–Schottky junction integrated with N-doped 2D carbon nanosheets (FeNi/FeNiO-NCS), is judiciously created. This architecture delivers high ORR, OER, and HER activity and durability, attributed to the Mott–Schottky effect and interface coupling. The FeNi/FeNiO-NCS-based Zn-air batteries and water electrolyzer manifest high efficiency and record-breaking cycling stability, surpassing previously reported trifunctional electrocatalysts in literature.

                                                                    Polymer‐Mediated Confined Assembly of High‐Entropy Perovskite Hydroxide Nanocubes for Ammonia Production and Zinc–Nitrate Battery Energy Supply
                                                                    Chaofan Liu, Tian Xie, Guohao Xue, Xiaobo Xu, Hele Guo, Yawen Guan, Tianlu Wang, Ziyuan Rao, Nan Zhang, Tianxi Liu, Claire J. Carmalt, Johan Hofkens, Xiaoqin Zeng, Feili Lai

                                                                    e25869 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                    A polyvinylpyrrolidone-mediated confined assembly strategy enables the synthesis of nanocubic high-entropy perovskite hydroxides with optimized active site exposure, achieving efficient ammonia production and high energy performance in the zinc-nitrate battery.

                                                                      3D Printing of Continuous Helical Carbon Fiber Reinforced Polylactic Acid Composites, Exhibiting Integrated Mechanical, Actuating, and Sensing Multifunctional Synergistic Properties
                                                                      Zirui Liu, Chang Liu, Jiuqi Sun, Xuliang Lu, Rilong Wu, Zhaohua Lin, Hao Zhang, Suqian Ma, Yunhong Liang

                                                                      e17291 | First Published: 20 November 2025

                                                                      Inspired by the silkworm silk-spinning process, a design and 3D printing manufacturing method for continuous helical twisted carbon fiber reinforced polylactic acid composite material is proposed. The unique continuous carbon fiber single-bundle and multi-bundle helical twisting process enhances the mechanical strength of the material while achieving the integrated and synergistic functions of actuation and sensing.

                                                                        Multi‐Shell Layer Heterostructure Construction of Co/Ni3ZnC0.7/C Composite with Built‐In Electric Field for Efficient Electromagnetic Response
                                                                        Ruilin Liu, Xiaomeng Jiang, Cui Ni, Baolei Wang, Chuanxin Hou, Xiaoyang Yang, Yuping Zhang, Wei Du, Xiubo Xie

                                                                        e23317 | First Published: 22 November 2025

                                                                        Through the visualization of interface charge distribution and magnetic flux lines, the multishell structure Co/Ni3ZnC0.7/C composite can generate a BIEF and magnetic coupling effects. The synergy of magnetic coupling, polarization, and conduction losses achieves an EAB of 5.64 GHz, which is further extended to an ultra-wide 9.63 GHz through metamaterial design.

                                                                          Enhancing Lithium‐Ion Transport in Polymer Plastic Crystal Electrolytes via Formation of Physically Dynamic Networks
                                                                          Bitgaram Kim, Min Kim, Yun Chan Kang, Ji-Hun Seo

                                                                          e24564 | First Published: 23 November 2025

                                                                          A physically dynamic polyrotaxane network preserves the molecular mobility of succinonitrile that is typically suppressed when succinonitrile is confined in rigid polymer networks. Local sliding and rotation at threaded α-cyclodextrin junctions enhance the ionic conductivity and promote a uniform, stable lithium interface, enabling stable Li metal cell operation.

                                                                            Electron Island Effect: Cross‐Layer Embedded Quantum Dot Heterostructures Toward Ultrahigh‐Energy‐Density Alkaline Aqueous Battery
                                                                            Yuying Zhao, Fengming Zhou, Ruihan Gao, Wenfeng Jiao, Zexing Wu, Jingqi Chi, Qi Zhang, Zhi Su, Zhenyu Xiao, Lei Wang

                                                                            e19910 | First Published: 20 November 2025

                                                                            An innovative electrochemically-driven in situ etching strategy is reported to uniform cross-layer embedded Co9S8 quantum dots (Co9S8-QDs) into cobalt double hydroxide (Co9S8-QDs/LDH), creating abundant and strongly coupled hetero-interfaces. The strongly coupled Co9S8-QDs function as “electron islands”, withdrawing electrons of LDH matrix and significantly enhancing electron transfer efficiency and OH− adsorption, resulting in substantially improved electrochemical performance in Co9S8-QDs/LDH//rGO-Zn alkaline aqueous batteries.

                                                                              Programmable and Multi‐Responsive Actuators with Reversible Deformation Based on Al/CNTs‐PDMS/SiOx Composite Films for Bionic Applications and Energy Harvesting
                                                                              Zhijian Liao, Xin Kang, Zehui Lin, Xincheng Jiang, Shaofeng Zeng, Zhijie Lin, Nengbin Hua, Qiaohang Guo

                                                                              e22966 | First Published: 23 November 2025

                                                                              The Graphical Table of Contents showcases the fabrication of materials, their near-infrared (NIR) light-driven deformation, their bidirectional reversible deformation triggered by ethanol and organic solvents, a liquid-solid triboelectric nanogenerator (TENG), and biomimetic applications such as grippers, bionic actuation, and self-powered sensing system.

                                                                                Open Access
                                                                                Generating Cell Surface Nucleated Hydrogels with an Artificial Membrane‐Binding Transglutaminase
                                                                                Rosalia Cuahtecontzi Delint, Tao Li, Kapil D. Patel, Will H. Zhang, Ben M. Carter, Graham J. Day, Adam W. Perriman

                                                                                e19196 | First Published: 24 November 2025

                                                                                Cell-based therapies require advanced strategies to enhance cell delivery and bioactivity. Cell membrane engineering offers an avenue to impart new functions to delivered cells to boost their viability and function. Here, an artificial membrane-binding transglutaminase is generated and biophysically characterized. This construct could adhere to fibroblast membranes and grow elastic proteinaceous hydrogels off their surface.

                                                                                  Thermally Adaptive PVDF‐HFP/Ionic Liquid Electrolyte Enabling Stable SEI/CEI Formation in Sodium Metal Batteries
                                                                                  Maher Jabeen, Muhammad Ishaq, Shuzhi Zhao, Yixing Shen, Yana Li, Wei Xu, Qinggang He, Haiying Che, Zi-Feng Ma

                                                                                  e25343 | First Published: 16 January 2026

                                                                                  A thermally adaptive polymer electrolyte enables interfacial stabilization with a high-rate performance in sodium-metal batteries.

                                                                                    Self‐Confined Effects Induced by F─H Bonds Simultaneously Enhance Mechanical Strength, Conductivity, and Self‐Healability of Ionic Elastomers
                                                                                    Yujie Jia, Chengzhen Chu, Shichun Cao, Zekai Wu, Shijia Gu, Zhengchong Chen, Hongjun Zhang, Junfen Sun, Zhengwei You

                                                                                    e26628 | First Published: 10 December 2025

                                                                                    An effective and versatile strategy for simultaneously enhancing tensile strength, conductivity, and self-healability of ionic elastomers is developed by leveraging F─H bond for self-confined nanodomains formed in situ, resulting in the highest recorded mechanical strength, puncture energy, and fracture energy solid-state ionic elastomers thus far.

                                                                                      Sodium Metavanadate Chelation‐Assisted Chemical Bath Deposition to High‐Efficiency Antimony Selenosulfide Solar Cells
                                                                                      Yuchen Li, Yue Hu, Shuwei Sheng, Rongfeng Tang, Yawu He, Zequan Jiang, Xiaoqi Peng, Junjie Yang, Tao Chen

                                                                                      e25557 | First Published: 20 November 2025

                                                                                      In this study, we develop a sodium metavanadate (NaVO3) chelation-assisted strategy to control chemical bath deposition growth of antimony selenosulfide (Sb2(S,Se)3) thin films. This strategy obtains a film with larger grain size and flatter morphology and suppresses the formation of antimony oxide impurity phases. Ultimately, the Sb2(S,Se)3 device achieved a power conversion efficiency of 10.15%.

                                                                                        Coupling Polar Terminal Designation and Double‐Network Structural Construction of Hydrogel Electrolyte for Boosting Dendrite‐Free Aqueous Zinc‐Ion Batteries
                                                                                        Ziming Liu, Hongxin Wan, Junyuan Li, Xuke Zhang, Heng Zhang, Xiangcai Li, Yifei Shi, Dawei Sha, Genxi Yu, Chao Yan

                                                                                        e20721 | First Published: 20 November 2025

                                                                                        A covalently crosslinked double-network hydrogel modified with a strongly polar terminal group achieves an extremely strengthened toughness and homogeneous transmission channels structure, which exhibits outstanding capability to resist dendrite growth and induce uniform zinc deposition.

                                                                                          Open Access
                                                                                          Interconnected Porous Hydrogels with Tunable Anisotropy Through Aqueous Emulsion Bioprinting
                                                                                          Hugo Edgar-Vilar, Francisca P. Correia, Inês M. Freitas, Vítor M. Gaspar, João F. Mano

                                                                                          e21050 | First Published: 24 November 2025

                                                                                          A 3D bioprintable microporous bioink is developed using an aqueous two-phase system (ATPS) composed of extracellular matrix (ECM) mimetic biopolymers. The ATPS bioink enables the fabrication of interconnected porous architectures with up to 70% porosity, supporting long-term cell viability and 3D cell alignment, enabling a simultaneous generation of constructs with porosity and programmable anisotropic features across different scales.

                                                                                            Synergistic Co and P Co‐Doping in MoS2/NiO Heterostructures for Electronic Modulation Toward High‐Performance Alkaline Water Electrolysis
                                                                                            Hong Li, Jinshan Xia, Junjie Zheng, Xue Bai, Qinglin Ren, Weiju Hao, Qingyuan Bi, Guisheng Li, Jinchen Fan

                                                                                            e25383 | First Published: 24 November 2025

                                                                                            A heteroatom Co and P co-doping strategy is introduced to engineer the electronic structure of MoS2@NiO catalyst, improving the electrical conductivity and enhancing its intrinsic catalytic activity. An electrolyzer employing Co,P-MoS2@NiO/CC as a bifunctional catalyst achieves 1000 mA cm−2 at 1.82 V for water electrolysis and maintains stable operation for 100 h at 500 mA cm−2 without degradation.

                                                                                              Homogeneous Microphase Structure and Polymer‐Dominated Ion Transport Network Enable Durable Quasi‐Solid‐State Sodium Metal Batteries
                                                                                              Xin Chen, Huaian Zhao, Xueyan Zhang, Yuanheng Wang, Jiaxin Yan, Guangyu Cheng, Geping Yin, Chunyu Du, Chuankai Fu, Yunzhi Gao, Pengjian Zuo

                                                                                              e27023 | First Published: 17 December 2025

                                                                                              A novel quasi-solid-state electrolyte (QSSE) with high continuity and homogeneity is fabricated by introducing octavinyl polyhedral oligomeric silsesquioxane (OV-POSS) into the in situ polymerization. Owing to the polymer-dominated ion transport network, the designed QSSE demonstrates homogeneous ion flux, rapid charge transfer kinetics, and superior interfacial compatibility. The sodium metal batteries employing the novel QSSE exhibit significantly enhanced electrochemical performance.

                                                                                                Interface‐Orchestrated Dual Microenvironments in CDMOF/MXene for Selective Dye Adsorption and Lithium–Sulfur Battery Enhancement
                                                                                                Bingxin Sun, Xuan Chen, Yuxuan Jiang, Liwei Lin, Dan Wang, Rui Wang, Chenlong Feng, Guowang Diao, Yuanzhe Piao, Wang Zhang, Huan Pang

                                                                                                e26503 | First Published: 23 November 2025

                                                                                                A dual-microenvironment CDMOF/MXene hybrid is constructed via microwave-assisted in situ growth. The polar interfacial groups of MXene and the supramolecular cavities of CDMOF cavities synergistically enable selective recognition and catalytic conversion, achieving efficient MG adsorption and enhanced polysulfide regulation in Li–S batteries. The work provides a versatile platform for coupling pollutant removal with energy storage in a single material system.

                                                                                                  Poly(N‐isopropylacrylamide) Self‐Fabricated Coating for Marine Anti‐Biofouling
                                                                                                  Jiongyan Chen, Zhao Wang, Sining Li, Qingyi Xie, Chunfeng Ma, Guangzhao Zhang

                                                                                                  e25696 | First Published: 22 November 2025

                                                                                                  A novel self-fabricated antifouling coating for cooling pipeline systems is developed. The coating exhibits ease of operation, stability, anti-biofouling properties, and corrosion resistance. This study provides a new strategy for addressing the marine fouling issues of cooling pipeline systems.

                                                                                                    A Visible and NIR Light‐Driven Photoswitch With High Fluorescence On/Off Contrast and Near‐Quantitative Cyclization Yield for Photonic Applications
                                                                                                    Yangyang Wang, Yuqi Tang, Yuzhu Zheng, Yongliang Feng, Panpan Wang, Linyu Zhang, Ziyong Li, Hui Guo, Quan Li

                                                                                                    e25706 | First Published: 23 November 2025

                                                                                                    A novel dithienylethene (DTE) photoswitch with a donor-DTE-acceptor-donor (D-DTE-A-D′) architecture has been rationally designed and synthesized. By employing a sophisticated “acceptor synergistic conjugation system” strategy, the compound exhibits fast photoswitching kinetics, a near-quantitative cyclization yield (up to 96.6%), and an exceptional fluorescence ON/OFF ratio of 2300:1 and multicolor fluorescence switching behavior—achieved within just 5 seconds of irradiation with long-wavelength green light.

                                                                                                      Dynamic Na+ Bridges: A 3D Printing Strategy for Hydrogels With High Strength, Low Hysteresis, Strong Adhesion, and Self‐Healing
                                                                                                      Mengyao Chen, Wenjing Qin, Yanli Wang, Hanlin Zhai, Changshun Gu, Xiangchuan Zhao, Ying Bi, Yixin Xu, Zeyu Ming, Shengrong Li, Shu Hu, Xingyue Zhang, Xiaoye Ma, Shougen Yin

                                                                                                      e24993 | First Published: 23 November 2025

                                                                                                      This study develops a 3D-printed hydrogel via dynamic Na⁺ bridges, synergistically achieving high strength, strong adhesion, low hysteresis, and rapid self-healing. The Na⁺ ions enable dynamic bond recombination and electrostatic shielding, overcoming traditional performance trade-offs. Integrated machine learning further allows high-accuracy human motion recognition.

                                                                                                        Carbon Dot Bridging Effect Enables the Interweaving of Long‐ and Short‐Chain Polymers for Enhanced Hydrogel Performance
                                                                                                        Yihong Gao, Huanxin Huo, Yumeng Chen, Xin Duan, Hongshan Li, Wei Wu, Cuilian Qiu, Xu Ding, Huijun Zhang, Jinxian Liu, Guanben Du, Long Yang

                                                                                                        e24953 | First Published: 29 November 2025

                                                                                                        The researchers propose a novel strategy to construct amorphous polymer hydrogels by interweaving long and short chains through carbon dots bridging. This resulting DGC/PAM hydrogel achieves enhanced mechanical properties with a high tensile strength of 470 kPa, a toughness of 4.9 MJ m−3 and a strain of 2200%, and an excellent interfacial adhesion of 160 kPa.

                                                                                                          Spatially Separated Dual‐Probiotic Hydrogel Enables Synergistic Infection Control and Tissue Healing
                                                                                                          Shangdongze Wu, Jia Liu, Jiayi Hou, Lingjie Song, Yutong Bai, Haixu Dou, Chaoqun Li, Leisheng Zhang, Hoon Eui Jeong, Jie Zhao

                                                                                                          e24157 | First Published: 28 November 2025

                                                                                                          A dual-probiotic microsphere hydrogel featuring spatially segregated Lactobacillus reuteri and Lactobacillus fermentum is presented. The architecture enables controlled yet independent release of reuterin and antioxidant metabolites, achieving strong antibacterial efficacy (≈97.8% against E. coli, ≈98.5% against S. aureus) and robust ROS-scavenging (≈83.9%) without chemical additives. The system markedly accelerates infected wound healing, achieving complete closure within 8 days.

                                                                                                            Bioinspired Multi‐Scale Interfacial Architectonics for Synchronous Mechanical‐Environmental Stabilization of PPTA Fibers
                                                                                                            Ying Yuan, Fengdong Teng, Yutong Cao, Yan Wang, Zuming Hu, Na Li, Junrong Yu

                                                                                                            e24971 | First Published: 23 November 2025

                                                                                                            This study presents a molecular-engineered polybenzoxazine nanocoating on PPTA fibers. The coated fibers achieve multiscale property improvements with 25.69% Young's modulus elevation, 53.95% compressive modulus amplification, and 82.38% interfacial shear strengthening. The coating confers exceptional durability to PPTA fiber against cyclic stress, acid rain corrosion, and prolonged UV irradiation in extreme environments.

                                                                                                              Open Access
                                                                                                              Organometallic Precursor‐Induced Gradient Architecture on Multilayer Nanoporous Graphene Membranes for Precise Organic Solvent Nanofiltration
                                                                                                              Junhyeok Kang, Hwayong Lee, Suseong Hyun, Jiwon Kim, Ju Yeon Kim, Jeongyeob Oh, Yunseong Ji, Byungchan Han, Dae Woo Kim

                                                                                                              e26926 | First Published: 22 November 2025

                                                                                                              Alumina growth narrows surface pores and seals non-selective defects, enhancing selectivity while preserving the nanoporous graphene architecture. Additionally, the deposition enables gradient-controlled structural modification, with intergrown alumina acting as a physical cross-linker that stabilizes the laminar structure. This dual effect, pore refinement and interlayer reinforcement, allows precise molecular weight cut-off (MWCO) tuning with ultrafast organic solvent permeance.

                                                                                                                Electronically Modulated Defective Cu1.81S by FeN4 for High‐Performance Nitroarene Hydrogenation
                                                                                                                Zhenxing Ren, Lizhen Lian, Xingle Zuo, Xinkang Cui, Min Zhang, Liu Deng, Liqiang Wang, You-Nian Liu

                                                                                                                e24811 | First Published: 22 November 2025

                                                                                                                Cu-based catalysts underperform in three-phase nitroarene hydrogenation due to weak H2 activation. A dual-site catalyst is constructed that couples FeN4 with defect-rich Cu1.81S on porous carbon. The electron transfer from FeN4 to Cu1.81S, combined with abundant defects, reduces the H2 dissociation energy barrier at the Cu sites, enabling >99% aniline yield and a TOF of 625 h−1 at 120 °C under 10 bar of H2.

                                                                                                                  Orientation Control of Metal–Organic Framework Membranes Through Magnetic Induced Room‐Temperature Growth
                                                                                                                  Fujun Li, Zeyu Cao, Hui Zhang, Feichao Wu, Weijuan Guo, Yanqing Yang, Jingde Li, Xiongfu Zhang, Yi Liu

                                                                                                                  e25582 | First Published: 22 November 2025

                                                                                                                  The applied magnetic field drives Co2+ ions to orderly coordinate with ligands to expose the lowest-energy (200) crystal plane of Co-based zeolitic imidazolate framework-L (Co-ZIF-L) crystals, and the orientation of resulting MOF membranes can be regulated by changing magnetic field directions.

                                                                                                                    Target‐Induced Dye‐Sensitized Solar Cell Design on Photoelectrochemical Anode for Improved Enantiomer Discrimination
                                                                                                                    Shujia Wang, Jian-Hong Zhu, Yulin Zheng, Zhida Gao, Yan-Yan Song

                                                                                                                    e23978 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                    A target-induced DSSC-PEC is developed on a chiral TiO2-nanotubes photoanode for enantiomer discrimination. L-/D-DOPA recognition provided the growth of PtNC cocatalysts and the bridge of N719 dye, by which visible-light harvesting and electron transfer are enhanced; the I−/I3− couple is employed for dye regeneration and signal stability. This target-recognition-induced DSSC-PEC system shows high enantioselectivity and offers significant advantages over traditional MOSs-based PEC sensors.

                                                                                                                      Constructing a Highly Salt‐Dissociated Interface Via a Salt‐Rich Polyurea Coating for Advanced LiNi0.9Co0.05Mn0.05O2 Cathode
                                                                                                                      Sihan Li, Yunhai Zhang, Le Zhao, Hongbin Zhang, Linna Che, Wenzhang Lv, Zhouhao Fu, Jie Tan, Yixin Li, Jun Bao, Qi Zhang, Dan Sun, Yougen Tang, Haiyan Wang

                                                                                                                      e23573 | First Published: 28 November 2025

                                                                                                                      A salt-rich polyurea coating is constructed on nickel-rich cathodes. This layer suppresses interfacial degradation and enables rapid Li⁺ transport via a unique polymer-salt coupling. The flexible coating also inhibits microcracks by accommodating volume changes. The modified cathode exhibits significantly improved cycling stability and high reliability under harsh conditions, enabling long-life lithium-ion and solid-state batteries.

                                                                                                                        Asymmetric C─C Coupling Induced by a Potential Field Boosts Electrocatalytic CO2 Reduction to Multi‐Carbon Products
                                                                                                                        Sihang Hu, Haiquan Liu, Mingyu Yang, Huan Dai, Chongyang Tang, Dong He, Xiangheng Xiao

                                                                                                                        e22128 | First Published: 30 November 2025

                                                                                                                        Thiol modification creates a potential field on the surface of Cu-based catalysts, which enhances CO2 mass transfer, facilitates its activation and hydrogenation to *COH, and promotes asymmetric *CO-*COH coupling at the Cu(100)/(111) interface. This enables high Faradaic efficiency (73.79%) for ethylene production at industrial current densities (>400 mA cm−2).

                                                                                                                          Ultrastable Oleophilic PTFE Channel with Wedge Arrays by Femtosecond Laser for Self‐Transport of Oily Droplets in Harsh Environments
                                                                                                                          Zehang Cui, Chenghong Guo, Guoqiang Li, Yuan Wang, Dongxu Xiao, Haojie Xu, Yiyu Chen, Haoyu Bai, Moyuan Cao

                                                                                                                          e24539 | First Published: 24 November 2025

                                                                                                                          A biomimetic ultrastable oleophilic two-order wedge channel fabricated from PTFE enables rapid, anti-gravity transport of low-surface-tension oils in air and underwater. It demonstrates exceptional chemical corrosion and abrasion resistance, outperforming conventional materials, and allows for selective oil separation, reconfigurable transport, and efficient droplet collection for use in demanding microfluidic applications.

                                                                                                                            Bioinspired Superhydrophobic and Conductive Janus Asymmetric Triboelectric Materials for Intelligent Tactile Perception
                                                                                                                            Xuelian Zou, Tao Liu, Huiya Zhang, Jinlong Wang, Kang Yu, Qiguan Luo, Wenxia Zhai, Siyi Qin, Xiaochen Pan, Zewen Dang, Shuangfei Wang, Shuangxi Nie, Zhiwei Wang

                                                                                                                            e26356 | First Published: 22 November 2025

                                                                                                                            This study introduces a bioinspired hierarchical Janus triboelectric material (HJTM) that integrates superhydrophobic protection, conductive sensing, and machine learning-assisted recognition. The material retains 95.32% of its performance under 99% relative humidity and achieves high-accuracy object recognition (accuracy ≥ 95.0%). This work provides a new pathway toward stable, self-powered tactile perception in complex environments.

                                                                                                                              Self‐Organized Metal‐Carbon Multilayers Formed by Ion Beam Induced Deposition from Trimethyl(methylcyclopentadienyl)platinum(IV)
                                                                                                                              Patrick M. Eckhert, Johnathon M. Johnson, Lisa McElwee-White, David Howard Fairbrother

                                                                                                                              e19982 | First Published: 19 December 2025

                                                                                                                              Ion beam induced deposition (IBID) of MeCpPtMe3 using low-energy Ar+ and H2+ generates self-organized multilayers of Pt and C, in marked contrast to the homogeneous deposits created by typical IBID and EBID. The Pt and C layer thickness is ≈8 nm with Pt layers consisting of 8–10 nm grains, with C layers containing smaller 2–3 nm Pt grains.

                                                                                                                                Dielectric Modulation Based on TiO2 Phase Transition Engineering
                                                                                                                                Yun-Xia Bai, Bo Cai, Lu Zhou, Martin C. Koo, Pei-Yan Zhao, Zhi-Ling Hou, Da-Peng Liu, Guang-Sheng Wang

                                                                                                                                e26359 | First Published: 24 November 2025

                                                                                                                                This work regulate the dielectric constant of TiO2 by engineering phase transitions based on the differences in electrical conductivity and Fermi level between rutile and anatase phases.The optimized C-Ni/TiO2 sample achieved excellent performance (RLmin = −62.2 dB, EAB = 6.2 GHz in 2.2 mm) This work provides valuable insights into the regulation of dielectric parameters through phase transition engineering.

                                                                                                                                  Giant Flexoelectricity in Silicon via Light‐Sensitive Schottky Junction
                                                                                                                                  Tingjun Wang, Xing'an Jiang, Yida Yang, Yu Lei, Yuanyuan Cui, Chunyang Li, Guangcheng Wang, Jing Zhao, Xiaolei Wang, Xueyun Wang, Yingzhuo Lun, Jiawang Hong

                                                                                                                                  e21436 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                                  Flexoelectricity can endow silicon with electromechanical properties, but it is intrinsically too weak. It is enhanced by six orders of magnitude via Schottky junctions and light modulation, achieving a flexoelectric coefficient up to 1 mC m−1. A prototype energy harvester delivers a normalized power density of 2.5 µW cm3 g2 Hz−1, highlighting silicon's potential for high-performance applications.

                                                                                                                                    Bionic Soft Anisotropic Metamaterials for Replicating Tissue‐Like Large‐Deformation Mechanics under Diverse Loadings
                                                                                                                                    Jinyu Ji, Kai Zhang, Xiaogang Guo, Daining Fang

                                                                                                                                    e20564 | First Published: 23 November 2025

                                                                                                                                    A class of Bionic Soft Anisotropic Metamaterials (BSAMs) is developed to replicate the complex, large-deformation anisotropic mechanics of soft biological tissues under multiple loading conditions. Using a neural network–assisted inverse design strategy, BSAMs achieve simultaneous mechanical matching under uniaxial, biaxial, and shear loadings across diverse tissue types. This bioinspired framework provides a versatile platform for tissue-mimetic engineering, soft robotics, and bio-integrated devices.

                                                                                                                                      Open Access
                                                                                                                                      Silver Ion‐Mediated [hk1]‐Oriented Sb2Se3 Crystal Growth for Efficient Photoelectrochemical Hydrogen Evolution
                                                                                                                                      Ziying Zhang, Zhenbin Wang, Mirjana Dimitrievska, Miloš Baljozović, Yongping Gan, Pardis Adams, Sanghyun Bae, Thomas Moehl, Wenzhe Niu, Xiang jiu Guan, S. David Tilley

                                                                                                                                      e24173 | First Published: 29 November 2025

                                                                                                                                      Ag+-mediated hydrothermal crystal engineering promotes preferential [hk1]-oriented growth of Sb2Se3 via an ultrathin MoOx interlayer, improving crystallinity and suppressing non-radiative recombination. The optimized Ag+ treatment photocathode delivers 24.7 mA cm−2 at 0 VRHE and improved stability, revealing an ion-modulated route to high-performance Sb2Se3-based photoelectrodes for efficient photoelectrochemical hydrogen evolution.

                                                                                                                                        In Situ Construction of Rich‐LiF/LiBr SEI by Ion‐Supported Porous Polymer Strategy for High Interfacial Stability All‐Solid‐State Lithium Metal Batteries
                                                                                                                                        Jintao Liu, Ruijie Mao, Tanghao Wang, Xiao Liu, Yibing Qu, Biao Zhang

                                                                                                                                        e23301 | First Published: 27 November 2025

                                                                                                                                        Ion-supported porous polymer were synthesized and used to construct rich-LiF/LiBr SEI layer. The Br− in porous polymer can prevent covalent bond breakage, thereby making the formation of LiBr easier. At the same time, the cations in porous polymer can catalyze breaking of C─F bonds in TFSI– to form more LiF.

                                                                                                                                          Open Access
                                                                                                                                          Near‐Infrared Light‐Programmable Negative Differential Transconductance in Organic Electrochemical Transistors for Reconfigurable Logic
                                                                                                                                          Debdatta Panigrahi, Christina J. Kousseff, Aristea Pavlou, Zeinab Hamid, Iain McCulloch, Fabrizio Torricelli, Paul W. M. Blom, Paschalis Gkoupidenis

                                                                                                                                          e25271 | First Published: 24 November 2025

                                                                                                                                          Organic electrochemical transistors based on a Near-Infrared (NIR)-responsive polymer p(C4DPP-T) and iodide electrolyte exhibit optically programmable negative differential transconductance. NIR illumination triggers an iodine-mediated redox process, enabling a transition from binary to ternary conductance states within a single-layer device. This light-driven tunability enables reconfigurable logic operation programmable directly by optical stimuli.

                                                                                                                                            Phase‐Guided Assembly of RuO2 Epitaxial Films on TiO2 Nanofibers: Interfacial Coherence Outperforming Defect‐Driven Mismatch for Active Site Generation in Li–O2 Batteries
                                                                                                                                            Seungho Jung, Myeong-Chang Sung, Bobae Ju, Hyunseok Yoon, Yumin Park, Dong-Wan Kim

                                                                                                                                            e24403 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                                            The RuO2@TiO2 catalyst, featuring epitaxial nanofilms on rutile TiO2, demonstrates superior Li-O2 battery performance over the nanocluster-based RuO2@TiO2. This enhancement is driven by phase-controlled interfacial coherence, which promotes oxygen vacancy formation and maximizes noble metal utilization, leading to high capacity and excellent cycling stability.

                                                                                                                                              Confined Construction of Artificial Electron Transfer Pathways in Cell Wall Mesopores for Enhanced Oxygen Reduction Reaction at Microbial Cathodes
                                                                                                                                              Tianhao Zhao, Zhenkai Feng, Libo Wang, Jialiang Pan, Qing Zhang, Lin Yang, Jun Lu, Zhengyu Bai

                                                                                                                                              e23907 | First Published: 08 December 2025

                                                                                                                                              A confinement strategy for the construction of artificial electron transfer pathways within the cell wall mesopore is reported. Compare with conventional surface polymerization, this strategy substantially increases electron flux through the respiratory chain, markedly accelerating the reaction kinetics of terminal oxidases. Consequently, the ND@Glu-PDA nanohybrid cell exhibits significantly enhanced electrocatalytic performance for oxygen reduction.

                                                                                                                                                Synergistic and Continuous Coordination–Adsorption Modulation via Zinc Picolinate for Dendrite‐Free and High‐Rate Zinc Metal Anodes

                                                                                                                                                e27040 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                Zinc picolinate (Zn(Pic)2) additive enables dendrite-free Zn deposition via dual functions. Pic− coordinates Zn2⁺ for facilitated ion delivery, and adsorbs on the electrode, inducing epitaxial growth along the (002) plane through synergistic molecular interactions, effectively passivating high-energy facets.

                                                                                                                                                  Special Adsorption Additive Enabling Robust Interphases for Thermal‐Resilient Lithium Metal Batteries
                                                                                                                                                  Dong Yang, Zengjiong Gao, Haotian Xie, Farwa Mushtaq, Zhongpeng Chen, Yanghua Li, Yuchen Wang, Meng Zhang, Kewang Yang, Guang Zhang, Yucheng Wen, Meinan Liu

                                                                                                                                                  e23015 | First Published: 10 December 2025

                                                                                                                                                  An advanced 2,5-dichlorothiophene (DCT) additive with specific adsorption is developed for restructuring the electric double layer and regulating interphase chemistry to boost Li+ diffusion kinetics at the interphase, empowering high-performance LMBs even under high temperature.
