                                                                                                                                                    Inspired by the amphiphilic structure of cell membrane phospholipids. Here, a bionic hydrated nanodomains (BHNs) hydrated eutectic electrolyte strategy to realize high-δ-MnO2-loading Zn.

                                                                                                                                                      In‐Sensor Reservoir Computing System Based on Multifunctional Optoelectronic Synaptic Transistor for Efficient Human Activity Recognition
                                                                                                                                                      Cong Peng, Weidong Xie, Enlong li, Di Liu, Xifeng Li, Huipeng Chen

                                                                                                                                                      e23188 | First Published: 28 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                      A multifunctional opto-electronic synaptic transistor (OEST) is developed. The OEST can seamlessly switch between volatile and non-volatile functions via optical input and gate modulation.

                                                                                                                                                        Targeted Etching of Heterogeneous Phases and Crystal‐Induced Regrowth for Direct Single‐Crystal Regeneration of Spent Cathode Materials
                                                                                                                                                        Baotong Cao, Jinhua Shi, Qingfeng Liu, Fanbin Hu, Haigang Dong, Zitong Fei, Qi Meng, Peng Dong

                                                                                                                                                        e27055 | First Published: 22 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                        This study proposes a synergistic regeneration strategy utilizing targeted etching of heterogeneous phases and crystal-induced regrowth to achieve performance upgrade of spent polycrystalline materials into single-crystal material. The regenerated NCM exhibits significantly reduced cation mixing (from 13.23% to 1.38%), eliminated rock-salt phase barriers, and enhanced interfacial stability, delivering an initial discharge specific capacity of 183.60 mAh·g−1 at 1C and a capacity retention of 86.81% after 200 cycles. This work provides a feasible pathway for direct single-crystal regeneration of spent cathode materials through etching and structural reconstruction.

                                                                                                                                                          Reconstructing Short‐Hydrogen‐Bond Network via Hexametaphosphate Boosts Chloride‐Ion Shielding Effect Enhancement in Seawater Electrolysis
                                                                                                                                                          Zemin Sun, Lanke Luo, Shuo Wang, Lian Zhao, Yaping Chen, Qingsong Hua, Liu Lin, Zhenhua Yan

                                                                                                                                                          e26825 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                          Focusing on addressing the bottleneck of oxygen evolution reaction (OER) catalysts in seawater splitting caused by side reactions from chloride ions (Cl−), this work explores coupling hexametaphosphate (HMP) with NiFe-LDH (NFL) to reconstruct short-range hydrogen-bond networks. It verifies HMP-NFL's superior chlorine resistance and long-term stability, and highlights the strategy's value for efficient seawater electrolysis.

                                                                                                                                                            Full‐Lifecycle Monitoring of Electrode States in Lithium‐Ion Batteries
                                                                                                                                                            Shuoyuan Mao, Yu Wang, Yao Lu, Ziding Wang, Depeng Wang, Xuebing Han, Yuejiu Zheng, Xuning Feng, Jianfeng Hua, Minggao Ouyang

                                                                                                                                                            e26132 | First Published: 27 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                            A long-life intercalation-type electrode potential sensor (EPS) is developed enabling full-lifecycle monitoring of lithium-ion battery electrodes states. By revealing and correcting reversible lithium loss through drift correction and secondary activation, the EPS maintains stable potential tracking over 1800 cycles—six times longer than previous designs, laying the foundation for electrode-level management in next-generation smart batteries.

                                                                                                                                                              High–Performance Polarity–Reconfigurable Transistor and Photodiode via Asymmetric Transferred Schottky and Evaporated Ohmic Contacts
                                                                                                                                                              Minh Chien Nguyen, Vu Khac Dat, Ngoc Thanh Duong, Van Dam Do, Van Tu Vu, Whan Kyun Kim, Anthony Cabanillas, Huamin Li, Woo Jong Yu

                                                                                                                                                              e27192 | First Published: 02 December 2025

                                                                                                                                                              Polarity-reconfigurable device (PRD) is demonstrated by integrating asymmetric contacts: transferred Schottky contact forming a high Schottky barrier and evaporated contact forming an Ohmic barrier. Drain/gate biases program four modes (n-/p-FET, intrinsic–n–type (I–N)/intrinsic–p–type (I–P) photodiode) via unipolar injection, achieving on/off >106, rectification >106, ≈3 µs response, enabling complementary logic (inverter and reconfigurable NAND/NOR gate) and in-sensor convolutional image processing.

                                                                                                                                                                Electrothermally Catalytic CO2 Selective Hydrogenation to Methanol on Electric Field‐Responsive Cu/in2O3 Catalyst
                                                                                                                                                                Qi Li, Tong Wu, Senlin Deng, Jie Li, Hui Zhao, Gang Wang, Chunshan Li

                                                                                                                                                                e29880 | First Published: 18 December 2025

                                                                                                                                                                The applied electric field triggers the favorable changes of physicochemical properties including redox behavior, CO2 adsorption capacity and oxygen vacancy content in the Cu/In2O3 catalyst, which consequentially promote CO2 efficient and selective hydrogenation to methanol.

                                                                                                                                                                  Freezing‐Induced Phase Separation and Conformational Inheritance of Macromolecules in Polyacrylonitrile Solution toward Advanced Porous Electrode Materials
                                                                                                                                                                  Yuanyou Peng, Meimei Yu, Lei Zhao, Huanzhong Zeng, Rui Liu, Guang Liu, Jiyi Sun, Fen Ran

                                                                                                                                                                  e24229 | First Published: 02 December 2025

                                                                                                                                                                  Polyacrylonitrile was fully dissolved in DMSO, a good solvent. After rapid freezing with liquid nitrogen, DMSO was removed in water to fix and inherit the random coil conformation of polyacrylonitrile. Finally, high electrochemical performance derived carbon electrode materials were prepared via preoxidation and carbonization heat treatment.

                                                                                                                                                                    Achieving Strong Corrosion Protection for Magnetic Refrigeration Materials via Electroplated Nickel Coatings
                                                                                                                                                                    Ruzhao Chen, Ziyuan Yu, Kaiming Qiao, Muhammad Zeeshan Naeem, Haodong Chen, Longlong Xie, Jiayi Sun, Jingyi Liu, Chenyu Xu, Haining Li, Caihua Gao, Ke Chu, Jiao Teng, Sergey Taskaev, Hu Zhang

                                                                                                                                                                    e26039 | First Published: 22 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                    This research proposes a Ni-electroplating method to significantly improve the corrosion resistance while remain the high magnetocaloric effect in typical La(Fe, Si)13-based (LFS) materials. The thermal conductivity also improves 3 times higher than the one before coating. LFS/Ni exhibits excellent comprehensive performance compared with other typical magnetocaloric materials, suggesting Ni-electroplating as an efficient method to promote the magnetic refrigeration application.

                                                                                                                                                                      Axial and Planar Electronic Modulation of Ni Single‐Atoms to Enhance the Ni─C Bonding Strength for Neutral CO2 Electroreduction
                                                                                                                                                                      Longlong Qi, Haiyang Li, Qiang Zhang, Xuan Xu, Baocang Liu, Lei Li, Peng Jing, Jun Zhang

                                                                                                                                                                      e23350 | First Published: 30 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                      The axial coordination by Cl and the planar second-shell coordination by P effectively enrich the charge density of Ni SAs. This enhances the adsorption for *COOH which is a key intermediate for CO product. The catalyst shows a partial current density for CO of −504 mA cm−2 at −1.4 V versus reversible hydrogen electrode in neutral electrolytes.

                                                                                                                                                                        Conquering Zn Dendrites via Crystal‐Facet Manipulation and Water‐Repellent Interphase Construction
                                                                                                                                                                        Hao Wu, Hong-Ting Yin, Shun Yao, Jin-Lin Yang, Ruiping Liu

                                                                                                                                                                        e24792 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                        Zn dendrites and water-driven parasitic limit AZIBs. A hydrophobic facet-directed ASEI is created via cinnamaldehyde adsorption that passivates (002)/(100) and funnels Zn2+ deposition to (101), lowering plating barriers and corrosion. CA–Zn symmetric cells run >2000 h at 1 mA cm−2; Zn||NVO full cells retain >160 mAh g−1 after 1000 cycles at 1 A g−1.

                                                                                                                                                                          Intrinsic Ferromagnetism Coupling‐Driven the Spin Reconfiguration of Single‐Atom Ru for Enhanced Acidic Water Oxidation
                                                                                                                                                                          Jing Jin, Yifan Liu, Zhihang Liu, Xing Peng, Yingqiang Li, Libin Hao, Chao Liu, Wenyao Zhang, Yongsheng Fu, Xiaohong Jiang, Pan Xiong, Junwu Zhu

                                                                                                                                                                          e25644 | First Published: 23 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                          Stable spin-state regulation of Ru single atoms is achieved in ferromagnetic Ru─Mn5O8 catalysts for efficient acidic water oxidation. The favorable Ru4+-O-Mn2+ configuration drives Ru4+ from low-spin to high-spin through strong ferromagnetic coupling. Unpaired dπ electrons of high-spin Ru4+ enhanced π-donation to coordinated O2−, optimizing Ru─O orbital overlap and strengthening key intermediates binding. This spin-state transition endows Ru─Mn5O8 with excellent catalytic activity and structural stability.

                                                                                                                                                                            Bioinspired Amphibious Soft Robotics with Conformable Ultrasonic Array for Adaptive Defect Mapping and Terrain Reconstruction
                                                                                                                                                                            Zhongming Chen, Xiaopeng Wang, Jiaqiang Xu, Haotian Li, Hongwu Su, Qilin Hua, Guozhen Shen

                                                                                                                                                                            e22932 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                            Amphibious Ultrasonic Soft Robot (AUS-R) integrating distributed conformable ultrasonic arrays with gas-driven locomotion is designed for adaptive defect mapping and 3D terrain reconstruction in terrestrial and aquatic environments. Its symmetric four-tentacle architecture enables omnidirectional navigation in confined/submerged spaces. And central and tentacle-mounted ultrasonic arrays provide forward-looking imaging and boundary/lateral sensing, achieving real-time full-space perception for non-destructive testing and environmental monitoring.

                                                                                                                                                                              All‐Vapor‐Deposited FACs‐Perovskite Solar Modules with Power Conversion Efficiency of >19%
                                                                                                                                                                              Peiran Hou, Shenghan Hu, Junye Pan, Yuxi Zhang, Yanqing Zhu, Min Hu, Yi-Bing Cheng, Alexandr N. Simonov, Jianfeng Lu

                                                                                                                                                                              e25366 | First Published: 23 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                              This study developed an all-vapor deposition method for fabricating 10.0 cm2 perovskite solar modules, achieving over 19% efficiency. The success relied on a hole transport layer that facilitated the formation of high-quality, pinhole-free perovskite films. The same technique also produced semitransparent modules with 17.5% efficiency, which retained >85% of their performance after 1000 h, demonstrating its versatility for scalable production.

                                                                                                                                                                                Revitalizing Prussian Blue Cathodes: Dynamic Self‐Healing in Dilute Aqueous Electrolytes for Ultra‐Stable Potassium‐Ion Batteries
                                                                                                                                                                                Jingqi Wang, Yuanxu Wang, W. L. Qubie, Erqing Xie, Jiecai Fu, Bingan Lu

                                                                                                                                                                                e24824 | First Published: 28 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                A novel dynamic electrochemical self-healing strategy is introduced using a trace redox-active additive in a dilute aqueous electrolyte resolves Prussian blue analogue cathode instability from transition-metal dissolution for aqueous potassium-ion batteries. It enables ultra-long stability with nearly 100% capacity retention over 7000 cycles, delivering the highest capacity of 197 mAh g−1 at 0.1 A g−1.

                                                                                                                                                                                  Self‐Adhesive, Isotropic, and All‐Wood Transparent Room Temperature Phosphorescent Laminates
                                                                                                                                                                                  Cai Chen, Ni Xia, Yutong Zhou, Yaqun Liu, Wanting Qu, Guanghui Song, Jun Rao, Baozhong Lü, Gegu Chen, Feng Peng

                                                                                                                                                                                  e22965 | First Published: 24 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                  An isotropic self-adhesive all-wood laminate is developed by delignification, oxidation-reduction modification, and physical densification. Oxidation-reduction modification endows abundant high-density cross-linking points, while further self-assembly lamination enables forming a dense structure through strong hydrogen bond, leading to the transparent laminate with isotropic high mechanical properties, outstanding optical properties, and long afterglow emission simultaneously.

                                                                                                                                                                                    Near‐Theoretical Oxidation Resistance in Copper Powder Enabled by Scalable Plasma‐Assisted Few‐Layer Graphene Encapsulation
                                                                                                                                                                                    Tao Liao, Bin Liu, Hui Zhou, Bicheng Liu, Ruiyu Lu, Ao Fu, Yuankui Cao, Yong Liu

                                                                                                                                                                                    e15549 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                    The TaPPS-synthesized defect-engineered few-layer Graphene-coated Cu powder demonstrates exceptional oxidation resistance with a 458 °C onset temperature (130% increase over bare Cu) and achieves 4.38 kg h−1 production. The dual-functional graphene armor simultaneously inhibits surface oxidation and blocks interfacial diffusion (maintaining 70% conductivity after 300 °C oxidation for 1 h).

                                                                                                                                                                                      Ultrastable Ni─Fe─P‐Based Heterostructures for Seawater Electrolysis
                                                                                                                                                                                      Huangcong Tang, Jieting Ding, Zemin Feng, Kui Shen, Liyu Chen, Yingwei Li

                                                                                                                                                                                      e28467 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                      NiTe nanorod arrays decorated with Ni2FeP create a built-in electric field effect at the interface to facilitate electron transfer from Ni2FeP to NiTe, which enhances Cl−-resistent ability and promotes the transformation of Ni2FeP to catalytically active γ-Ni(Fe)OOH species.

                                                                                                                                                                                        A Wearable Fiber Sensor Employing MXene/In2O3 Composites for NH3 Detection in Kidney Disease Diagnosis
                                                                                                                                                                                        Jiabei Li, Shuo Li, Lijuan Xie, Ziyao Wang, Xi Yang, Donghang Li, Xiaohong Li

                                                                                                                                                                                        e24182 | First Published: 27 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                        A flexible MXene/In2O3 fiber sensor demonstrates reliable ammonia detection in exhaled breath, enabling noninvasive screening for kidney disease. The sensor maintains high sensitivity under humid conditions and integrates seamlessly into a wearable mask. Combined with machine learning analysis, it achieves 94% accuracy, highlighting its potential for real-time, breath-based health monitoring.

                                                                                                                                                                                          A One‐Two Punch Strategy Against Cancer: Simultaneously Inducing and Eliminating Senescent Tumor Cells
                                                                                                                                                                                          Bo Fang, Fei Pan, Sailing Chen, Wenli Tian, Yang Zhu, Yaobang Shen, Hao Wang, Hui Guo, Yuan Ding, Yuhao Wang, Gaopeng Li, Daqian Xu, Zhongquan Sun, Xiao Li, Zhengwei Mao

                                                                                                                                                                                          e24946 | First Published: 27 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                          This study presents a novel “one-two punch” strategy for colon cancer treatment using metal-polyphenol network nanoparticles. These nanoparticles induce cellular senescence in tumor cells and then selectively eliminate these senescent cells, resulting in significant tumor growth suppression. The dual pH- and GSH-responsive design ensures high therapeutic efficacy and safety, offering a promising new paradigm for cancer treatment.

                                                                                                                                                                                            A MOF‐based Core–Shell System Enables Controlled Release and Ordered Assembly of Nano‐Hydroxyapatite for Enamel Remineralization
                                                                                                                                                                                            Huimin Liang, Ying Jia, Zhenyu Zhou

                                                                                                                                                                                            e27093 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                            A MOF-based core–shell system, ZIF-8@nHA, is developed to enable the controlled release and oriented assembly of nano-hydroxyapatite (nHA), achieving superior remineralization of enamel-like structures. Furthermore, ZIF-8@nHA affords facile preparation with economic feasibility, allowing direct coating onto invisible orthodontic appliances (ZIF-8@nHA@OA), highlighting significant clinical potential for effective treatment of orthodontic-induced enamel demineralization.

                                                                                                                                                                                              Capacitive Amplification toward Boltzmann‐Limit Switching in WSe2 Reconfigurable Field‐Effect Transistors
                                                                                                                                                                                              Jiali Yi, Qin Shuai, Guangcheng Wu, Haifeng Wu, Chenguang Zhu, Xingxia Sun, Qijun Zong, Yihan Liao, Xiaoli Zhu, Xiulian Fan, Zhengwei Zhang, Yu Zhou, Xiao Wang, Dong Li, Anlian Pan

                                                                                                                                                                                              e24990 | First Published: 28 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                              A WSe2 transistor employs a mirror floating gate to keep the sub-threshold swing at the Boltzmann limit of 60 mV dec−1, while a side polarity gate freely converts contacts between n-type and p-type. This design achieves a steep-slope device with reconfigurable carrier polarity, enabling fabricated CMOS circuits to deliver a gain of 213 times at a supply voltage of 1 V.

                                                                                                                                                                                                Open Access
                                                                                                                                                                                                Tin‐Oxo Nanocluster Extreme UV Photoresists Equipped with Chemical Features for Atmospheric Stability and High EUV Sensitivity
                                                                                                                                                                                                Yejin Ku, Gayoung Kim, Minseung Kim, Hyungju Ahn, Jin-Kyun Lee, Jiho Kim, Byeong-Gyu Park, Dohyun Moon, Sangsul Lee, Seohyeon Lee, Yu Ha Jang, Byung Jun Jung, Hyunseok Kim, Changhyeon Lee, Su-Mi Hur, Ji Young Park, Chawon Koh, Tsunehiro Nishi, Hyun-Woo Kim

                                                                                                                                                                                                e25300 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                                Fluoroalkyl-functionalized tin–oxo nanoclusters (N-TOC6) enable robust pattern formation through ligand crosslinking under EUV exposure without thermal processing. Sn–F coordination mitigates the Lewis acidity of Sn centers, suppressing reactions with airborne molecules and improving post-exposure pattern stability. Chemical orthogonality with non-fluorinated TOCs allows bilayer stacking that promotes electron injection, enhancing sensitivity for high-NA EUV lithography.

                                                                                                                                                                                                  Acylsemicarbazide‐Based Supramolecular Liquid Crystal Elastomers: from Molecular Design to Melt‐Processing Actuators
                                                                                                                                                                                                  Zhixiang Dong, Yihai Wang, Jianxia Chen, Zhuang Ma, Jian Ding, Tuan Liu, Naisheng Jiang, Yuzhan Li

                                                                                                                                                                                                  e24643 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                                  Through molecular design, the density and strength of hydrogen bonds in supramolecular liquid crystal elastomers are precisely tuned, enabling controlled modulation of their thermal, mechanical, and actuation properties. This versatile platform allows for the fabrication of complex 3D actuators, scalable melt processing, and excellent recyclability, providing a sustainable pathway for high-performance soft actuating materials.

                                                                                                                                                                                                    Dynamic Breaking the Covalency of Ru─O Bonds via Electronic Asymmetry Engineering for Efficient pH‐Universal Hydrogen Evolution
                                                                                                                                                                                                    Jiawen Sun, Jiangwei Zhang, Jing Sun, Yi-Ru Hao, Hui Xue, Hongliang Dong, Zhonglong Zhao, Qin Wang

                                                                                                                                                                                                    e23596 | First Published: 04 December 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                                    In this study, the urchin-like RuSA─CoP electrocatalyst with O─Ru─P structural motifs is successfully synthesized via a chemical bond-mediated electronic asymmetry engineering. Experiments and DFT calculations reveal the dynamic adaptability of Ru─O coordination during the HER reaction process. Hence, RuSA─CoP requires only overpotentials of 15, 38, and 36 mV at 10 mA cm−2 in alkaline, acidic, and neutral media, respectively.

                                                                                                                                                                                                      Unveiling Octahedral Oxygen as the Dominant Active Site in Facet‐Engineered Co3O4 Electrocatalysts for Chlorine Evolution Reaction
                                                                                                                                                                                                      Xiangkun Zhang, Han Zhao, Yao Hu, Wen-Da Zhang, Qingna Gong, Jiangyong Liu, Liangliang Xu, Jing Wang, Xiaoping Yang, Xiaodong Yan

                                                                                                                                                                                                      e18684 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                                      This study highlights Co3O4-[110] as highly active electrocatalyst for chlorine evolution reaction (CER). The (110) facets possess the highest surface concentration of octahedral oxygen (Ooct), which is conclusively identified as the dominant active site for CER. The application of the distribution of relaxation times method provides a detailed analysis of the electrochemical impedance, offering deep insight into the reaction kinetics.

                                                                                                                                                                                                        Open Access
                                                                                                                                                                                                        PRELIVE: A Framework for Predicting Lipid Nanoparticles In Vivo Efficacy and Reducing Reliance on Animal Testing
                                                                                                                                                                                                        Belal I. Hanafy, Chuan-En Lu, Kai Liu, Audrey Gallud

                                                                                                                                                                                                        e25076 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                                        PREdicting LNP In Vivo Efficacy (PRELIVE) framework enables the prediction of lipid nanoparticle (LNPs) organ-specific delivery through dual modeling approaches. Composition-based models using formulation parameters and protein corona-based models using biological fingerprints both achieve high predictive accuracy across multiple organs. Interactive design spaces reveal organ-specific optimal formulations, supporting rational LNP development while reducing animal testing requirements.

                                                                                                                                                                                                          Open Access
                                                                                                                                                                                                          Controlled Movement of Soft Actuators using Multi‐Responsive Microgel Arrays and Microcirculatory Systems
                                                                                                                                                                                                          Nengjian Huang, Brennan P. Watts, Stephen A. Morin

                                                                                                                                                                                                          e21444 | First Published: 29 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                                          Microscale hydrogels (microgels) feature improved mass transport characteristics supportive of fast actuation and chemical tunability amenable to programmed stimuli response. A unique soft actuator architecture is realized by encapsulating microgels in soft microcirculatory systems which enable the convenient delivery of liquid stimuli for powering and controlling motion of the device.

                                                                                                                                                                                                            Open Access
                                                                                                                                                                                                            Mechanistic Insights into a Synergistic FeOx/Fe‐N4 System for Practical Nitrate Abatement with Value‐Added Ammonia Recovery
                                                                                                                                                                                                            Qiang Zhou, Hongzhi Liu, Shenglin Liu, Yuwei Liu, Mamiko Nakabayashi, Yunlong Xie, Naoya Shibata, Feng Gong, Jean-Jacques Delaunay

                                                                                                                                                                                                            e26110 | First Published: 24 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                                            This work provides a novel interpretation of the nitrate reduction mechanism on iron oxides (FeOx) by employing constant-potential density functional calculations and reports the design and synthesis of a robust and high-performance Fe3O4/Fe-N4-C catalyst with remarkable Faradaic efficiency, current density, and stability under practical reaction conditions.

                                                                                                                                                                                                              Fully Recyclable All‐Solid‐State Supercapacitors with High Stretching Stability and Room‐Temperature Self‐Healing Capability
                                                                                                                                                                                                              Tiejun Mao, Tianqi Li, Yixuan Li, Xingyuan Lu, Wenjie Wang, Junqi Sun

                                                                                                                                                                                                              e27000 | First Published: 28 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                                              Fully recyclable, stretchable, and self-healable all-solid-state supercapacitors (ASSCs) are constructed by sandwiching a reversibly cross-linked polyvinyl alcohol-based electrolyte between elastic polyurethane/MXene–carbon nanotube electrodes. They exhibit high capacitance and Coulombic efficiency, and can be fully disassembled to recover each component with high purity. The recovered components can be reassembled into new ASSCs, which retain their original electrochemical performance.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                Mechanically Robust Super‐Lubrication Polycarbonate‐Polyurethane (PCU) Enabled by Design of Dynamic Mechanics Network and Manufacture of Constrained Hydrogel Coating
                                                                                                                                                                                                                Bingbing Liu, Weiyi Zhao, Hao-zhe Xu, Jinshuai Zhang, Yunsong Kong, Yibo Chen, Xiaoduo Zhao, Pengbin Yin, Yong-min Liang, Bo Yu, Shuanhong Ma, Feng Zhou

                                                                                                                                                                                                                e14292 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                                                This work reports a mechanically robust and super-lubrication PCU material (PCU-UPy-hydrogel) through a synergistic strategy of network mechanics design via constructing multiple hydrogen bonds and interpenetrating surface lubrication modification by applying interface-constrained effects. Its lubricity and load-bearing performances obviously surpass most reported polymer-based artificial meniscus material systems, and are even comparable to those of natural meniscus tissue.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Lattice Confinement‐Steered Adaptive Crystal‐Field and Spin‐State Transition of Co Single‐Atom Sites for Robust Water Cleanup
                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Jia-Cheng E. Yang, Min-Ping Zhu, Chenghua Sun, Fu-Yi Huang, Xin-Yuan Zhou, Yu-Ming Zheng, Shaobin Wang

                                                                                                                                                                                                                  e26104 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                                                  A scalable “frustrated Lewis pairs”-assisted lattice confinement strategy is reported, enabling Co substitution of tetrahedral and octahedral Al in millimetric γ-Al2O3 to form single-atom site monolithic catalysts. The synergy adsorption of Co─Al sites toward peroxymonosulfate induces the crystal-field and spin state transition of Co─O─Al/Co─Al ligand-bonded high-spin tetrahedral Co(II), facilitating charge transport to concurrently produce 1O2 and SO4•− for sustained water purification.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Gradient‐Structured Hydrogel with Integrated Actuation and Strain Sensing for Biomimetic Robotics
                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Xin Jing, Yeqing Xu, Jiazhou Zeng, Peiyong Feng, Shitao Li, Hao-Yang Mi

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    e24820 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    A magnetic-field-induced assembly was proposed to create a gradient hydrogel with synergistic actuation and self-sensing. This intelligent material exhibits high conductivity, sensitivity, and robust actuation, enabling biomimetic robots that move and monitor their motion in real-time.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Open Access
                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Membrane Wrapping for High Capacity and Rapidly Switchable Adhesives
                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Yeunhee Kim, Camryn A. Mills, Michael D. Bartlett

                                                                                                                                                                                                                      e23862 | First Published: 27 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                                                      A membrane-wrapping mechanism enables high-capacity, rapidly switchable adhesion by coupling suction, friction, and adhesion. Broadband performance is achieved in dry and underwater conditions, allowing fast, reversible manipulation of objects of diverse sizes and shapes, from delicate eggs and berries to flat, complex, or heavy objects. These outcomes advance next-generation gripping, robotic manipulation, and bioinspired interface design.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Open Access
                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Self‐Refreshing Bi‐Based Active Sites for Efficient and Durable Electrochemical CO2 Reduction
                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Yuxuan Xiao, Xiaoling Lai, Jinxian Feng, Ziwen Feng, Wendi Zhang, Lun Li, Weng Fai IP, Hui Pan

                                                                                                                                                                                                                        e23592 | First Published: 26 November 2025

                                                                                                                                                                                                                        A self-refreshing Bi-based catalyst is developed through the fabrication of a novel basic bismuth nitrate pre-catalyst, which exhibits differential active sites of Bi2O2CO3 at low potentials and metallic Bi phases at high potentials, self-repairing capability, and thus wide-potential-window efficiency and ultralong durability toward electrochemical CO2 reduction reaction.
